implementability.notes: File structure and module responsibilities are clearly specified with pseudocode for grid logic, drag/swap, BFS solvability. CDN is correct (Phaser 3.60.0 jsdelivr). Risk: variable-size items (1x1, 1x2, 2x1, 2x2) make BFS solvability and swap logic significantly more complex — stages.js may exceed 300 lines with full implementation.

performance.notes: SVG DOM pool strategy and shape count limits (12 per item) are specified. No SVG <animate> in game objects (JS-driven). However, mixing Phaser Canvas renderer with DOM SVG elements creates sync/layout risks at 60fps on mid-range Android. This is the primary technical concern.

modularity.notes: 6 files with clear, non-overlapping responsibilities. config.js as single source of truth for constants. ads.js isolated. One-directional dependency flow. Minor concern: game.js handles grid, drag, audio, wave-end, and lives — may need splitting.

maintainability.notes: Naming conventions consistent. localStorage schema fully defined. Difficulty tables are data-driven. SVG templates as JS functions in config.js is clean and cacheable.

first_impression.notes: Premise is original and charming: sentient leftover tupperware surviving grocery day. Comedic inversion (getting eaten = victory) is memorable and shareable. Fridge door opening animation on menu load creates immediate delight.

core_loop_satisfaction.notes: Wave announcement → drag rearrange → survival check loop is tight (1-3 min sessions). Juice is well-specified: camera shake 3x ±8px, horrified SVG expression, 50% timescale slowdown, screen dim, red vignette, green pulse on safety. Swap discovery mechanic creates natural eureka moments.

replayability.notes: 7 difficulty tiers with distinct new mechanics per tier. Daily seeded challenges with unique modifiers (Frozen Friday, Mega Meal Prep). Meta-progression with cosmetics and power-up slots. Dual-fridge parallel survival at wave 31+ adds substantial depth for long-term players.

difficulty_curve.notes: Mathematically precise: timer = max(1200, 3000 - wave*100). Rest waves every 5th (+500ms, -30% items). Boss waves every 10th. 5-tier difficulty table with exact parameter ranges. Solvability guarantee prevents unwinnable states.

ad_integration.notes: 6 distinct ad placements: interstitial post-game-over (every 3rd), interstitial post-rest-wave (every 2nd rest), rewarded continue, rewarded double-FP, rewarded daily challenge unlock, banner on menu. Well over 2 natural breaks per session. Frequency caps prevent fatigue.

session_economy.notes: FP earn rates specified by wave tier (+5 early, +8 late, +50 daily). Ad doubler creates clear monetization moment. Shop has cosmetic-only top tier and gameplay power-up slots at accessible prices. 10-15 hour F2P completion window is healthy. Future IAP story ($0.99-$4.99) included.

launch_readiness.notes: Every screen specified with px dimensions. HUD element sizes defined. Browser compatibility table (Chrome 80+, Safari 14+, Samsung 14+, Firefox 90+). Success metrics with concrete targets (D1 42%+, D7 22%+, crash <1%). localStorage schema complete. Effectively a production-ready spec.

key_risks: Variable-size item grid (1x1, 1x2, 2x1, 2x2) makes BFS solvability check and swap logic complex — may require stages.js to exceed 300 lines or simplify to 1x1-only items in initial build
Phaser Canvas + DOM SVG hybrid rendering requires careful architecture to achieve 60fps on mid-range Android — consider all-Canvas or all-DOM approach
game.js scope is broad (grid, drag, audio, wave management, lives) — pre-plan line count before implementation
key_strengths: Exceptionally detailed spec: every screen, HUD element, animation timing, and sound synthesis is precisely defined
Original charming premise with strong emotional hooks (horrified face, getting eaten = victory)
Mathematical difficulty scaling prevents vague specs — wave timer formula and difficulty tables are implementation-ready
6 ad placements with natural session integration and good frequency caps
Solvability guarantee prevents unwinnable states — shows deep design thinking
